title: myroxylon celastrinum
intro: Myroxylon celastrinum, also known as Palisander or Balsam of Peru, is a plant species in the legume family Fabaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to create a resin that has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Myroxylon celastrinum include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural insecticide and antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Myroxylon celastrinum include its resin being used in topical applications, such as ointments and creams, as well as being used in traditional medicine to create tinctures and infusions.
